intro: Grisebachianthus carsticola is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the mountainous regions of Cuba. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Grisebachianthus carsticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Grisebachianthus carsticola is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
